styling.rule: Use only utility classes provided by Tailwind. Do NOT create custom CSS classes, custom component classes, or named abstractions in stylesheets.
allowed: Tailwind utility classes directly in HTML (e.g., flex, gap-4, text-lg, bg-white)
Tailwind variants: responsive (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:), state (hover:, focus:, active:), dark mode (dark:)
@apply is forbidden — write utilities inline only
CSS custom properties (variables) inside :root via @theme in main.css for design tokens only (colors, fonts)
Minimal @layer base for resets or global element defaults if absolutely necessary
forbidden: Inline style= attributes unless driven by Alpine.js dynamic binding
Arbitrary values unless no standard scale value fits (e.g., w-[347px] is a last resort)

javascript.rule: All interactivity must be handled with Alpine.js directives directly in HTML. Avoid writing separate JS logic files for UI behavior unless absolutely necessary (e.g., third-party library initialization).

patterns.state: x-data on the nearest logical parent element
patterns.binding: x-bind or shorthand :attr for dynamic attributes
patterns.events: x-on or shorthand @event for event listeners
patterns.conditionals: x-show (preferred for toggle) or x-if (for true conditional rendering)

forbidden: Inline onclick= or other legacy event handlers

responsiveness.rule: Every layout and component must be fully responsive across all breakpoints. Mobile-first approach is required.
requirements: Start styling for mobile, then add md: / lg: overrides
Navigation must collapse to a hamburger menu on mobile (Alpine.js toggle)
Grid layouts must stack vertically on mobile (grid-cols-1) and expand on larger screens
Typography must scale appropriately (text-base mobile, text-lg+ desktop)
Touch targets must be at least 44x44px
Images must use w-full or max-w-* with responsive aspect ratios

design.philosophy: Clean, professional, and modern. Prioritize whitespace, clarity, and trust. This is a service business — the design must convey reliability and local expertise.

files.guttrdone.webp: Local gutter service site. Key patterns: dark navy/teal hero with large bold white uppercase headline; social proof badges (Google/Facebook star ratings) placed top-left of hero; circular/rounded image cutout floated to the right in the hero; two CTA buttons side-by-side (primary solid, secondary accent color); inline trust-points row below headline using small icons + short text; service cards with full-width image, rounded-bottom overlay with dark background and title, plus a CTA button at the bottom.
files.leith-heating-cooling.webp: HVAC service site — closest match to this project. Key patterns: full-width hero photograph (service van on location) as background; angled/chevron-shaped semi-transparent overlay on the left containing headline and two CTA buttons; bold orange accent bar directly below hero with 4 icon+text trust badges in a row; services section on a teal dotted-pattern background; service cards with white background, top photo, centered heading, short description, and orange CTA button.

apply: Hero must have a strong, high-contrast background (dark overlay on photo, or solid dark teal/navy)
Place a trust-badge strip (4 items with icons) immediately below the hero — use an accent color background (e.g., orange or red-orange) to make it pop
Service cards follow the image-top / title / description / CTA-button pattern
Use two visually distinct CTA button styles: primary (solid, brand color) and secondary (outlined or contrasting fill)
Navigation must show a prominent phone number CTA button on the right side (desktop)
Color palette: dark navy or teal as primary, red-orange or amber as accent, white for text on dark backgrounds
Section headings should be bold and use tracking-wide or uppercase for a strong, trustworthy feel

color_palette.primary: Dark navy or dark teal (e.g., #0a2540 or #0f4c6e) — used for hero, header, and dark sections
color_palette.accent: Red-orange or amber (e.g., #e84c1e or #f59e0b) — used for CTA buttons, highlight bars, and hover states
color_palette.neutral_light: Slate-50 or gray-100 — used for alternating section backgrounds

images.source: Use images from public/images/ for all visual content. Do not use external image URLs or placeholder services.
images.optimization: Always include width and height attributes on img tags. Use loading='lazy' for below-the-fold images. Use loading='eager' for hero/above-the-fold images.

typography.hierarchy: Maintain clear H1 → H2 → H3 hierarchy. Only one H1 per page.
typography.readability: Body text minimum text-base (16px). Line height leading-relaxed for paragraphs.
typography.headings: Section headings should be font-bold or font-extrabold, with tracking-wide. Hero H1 should be text-4xl md:text-5xl lg:text-6xl.

components: Use rounded corners (rounded-xl or rounded-2xl) for cards and containers
Use subtle shadows (shadow-md or shadow-lg) for depth — avoid harsh or flat styles
Buttons must have clear hover and focus states — use transition and duration-200 for smooth transitions
Section backgrounds should alternate (white / slate-50 / dark) to create visual rhythm
Trust-badge strip below hero: flex row, icon + short label per badge, accent background, full-width
Service cards: overflow-hidden rounded-2xl shadow-lg, img on top (aspect-video object-cover), content area with padding, CTA button at bottom

structure.rule: The page must be divided into clearly named semantic HTML sections. Each section maps to a content block from the content file.

sections.content: Main headline, subheadline, and primary CTA from content-homepage.md
4 trust/value pillars (18 years experience, complete care, customer-focused, local)
6 service cards: Heating Installation, AC Installation, Heating Repair, AC Repair, Maintenance, Indoor Air Quality
Logo or text list of supported brands
Comfort starts here — CTA to contact
License numbers and accepted payment methods

layout.header: Sticky navigation with logo, phone number (visible on desktop), and mobile hamburger menu
layout.footer: Company name, quick links, contact info, license info

content.source: All copy must come from content-*.md files in the project root. Do not invent or paraphrase content.

files.content-homepage.md: All text for the homepage sections

content.rule: If a content-*.md file does not cover a specific element (e.g., a button label), use short, clear, and professional English phrasing consistent with the existing content tone.

requirements: One <h1> per page — must match the primary page topic
Descriptive <title> tag: '[Page Topic] | Rick's AC Maintenance & Service LLC'
<meta name='description'> — 150-160 characters, includes primary keyword and location
Semantic HTML: use <header>, <nav>, <main>, <section>, <footer>, <article> appropriately
All images must have descriptive alt attributes
Internal anchor links must use descriptive text (not 'click here')
Use <address> tag for contact/location information
Structured data (JSON-LD) for LocalBusiness schema in <head>
All interactive elements must be keyboard-accessible
Focus styles must be visible — never use outline-none without a custom focus-visible: replacement
Color contrast ratio: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text
All form inputs must have associated <label> elements
Modals and drawers must trap focus and be closeable with Escape key
Use aria-label or aria-labelledby on landmark regions that lack a visible heading
Use role='button' + tabindex='0' if a non-button element is interactive
Skip-to-main-content link as the first focusable element in <body>
Navigation toggle button must have aria-expanded and aria-controls attributes

html: Validate that HTML is well-formed — no unclosed tags
Avoid deeply nested div-soup — prefer semantic elements
Comments are allowed for section demarcation (<!-- SECTION: Hero -->)
components: Reuse Handlebars partials in components/ for repeated structures (header, footer, modal)
Keep components focused — one responsibility per partial
performance: Minimize JavaScript — Alpine.js handles UI; avoid loading heavy libraries
Use Vite's build pipeline — do not add scripts via CDN links in production HTML

before_starting: Read the relevant content-*.md file to understand the copy
Review images in public/images/ to understand available visuals
Read existing components in components/ before creating new ones
when_building_a_section: Apply Tailwind utilities for layout and spacing
Add Alpine.js x-data only if the section has interactive state
Verify responsive behavior at mobile, md, and lg breakpoints
Check accessibility: alt text, aria attributes, keyboard access
do_not: Invent content not present in content-*.md
Break section structure without updating this file
